sessions.intent: Design in-memory SQLite datasource support for tests, builder, and PLang tests

before.assumptions: SqliteDataSource is the only IDataSource implementation. Actor creates it lazily with file path. No in-memory support exists in runtime2. Runtime1 had AppContext.SetSwitch pattern which is not OBP.
before.risk: Low — additive change. SqliteDataSource gains a new construction path, existing file-backed path unchanged.

sessions.plan: SqliteDataSource gains InMemory(name) static factory with sentinel connection. New Engine.Building object (like Testing) with IsEnabled. Actor.CreateDataSource() navigates to Engine.Testing.IsEnabled and Engine.Building.IsEnabled to decide. See .bot/runtime2-inmemory-datasource/architect/v1/plan.md for full spec.

actions.context: Read all relevant files to understand current DataSource system, Actor creation, Engine properties, and Testing/Debug patterns
Studied runtime1 in-memory patterns — AppContext.SetSwitch, InMemoryDbCache sentinel, ConvertToMemoryDb() — to understand what worked and what to avoid

Wrote coder handoff plan after iterative design discussion with Ingi. Key decisions: SQLite in-memory (not dictionary), sentinel on DataSource, Actor navigates Testing/Building context, new Building object on Engine.

after.notes: Design approved through conversation. Plan written and ready for coder handoff.

sessions.intent: Implement in-memory SQLite datasource with sentinel connection, Building object, and tests

before.assumptions: Architect plan is complete and approved. SqliteDataSource, Actor, Engine, GlobalUsings all reviewed. Test/Debug pattern understood for Building object.
before.risk: Low — additive change. All existing tests remain file-backed. New in-memory path is isolated.

sessions.plan: TDD approach: 1) Write 7 failing tests (InMemory CRUD, schema persistence, isolation, dispose, Actor integration with Testing/Building flags). 2) Implement SqliteDataSource.InMemory() with sentinel. 3) Create Engine/Build/this.cs. 4) Wire Building into Engine. 5) Add global alias. 6) Update Actor.CreateDataSource(). 7) Verify all tests pass.

actions.context: TDD: wrote 7 new tests first (InMemory CRUD, schema persistence, isolation, dispose, Actor integration). Verified compile failure (7 errors: InMemory not found, Building not found).
Added InMemory(name) static factory, private in-memory constructor with sentinel SqliteConnection, updated Dispose() to close sentinel before clearing pool.
New Building object following Debug/Test pattern. IsEnabled property, engine back-reference.
Added Building property (Build.@this type) and constructor init.
Added comment documenting Building can't be global alias (PLang.Building v1 conflict). Same pattern as Engine/CallStack.
Updated CreateDataSource() to check Engine.Testing.IsEnabled || Engine.Building.IsEnabled and use SqliteDataSource.InMemory() when true.

after.notes: All 1472 tests pass (1465 existing + 7 new). Building global alias couldn't be used due to PLang.Building namespace conflict — documented in GlobalUsings.cs.

sessions.intent: Review test quality for in-memory SQLite datasource implementation

before.assumptions: Coder v1 claims 1472 tests pass with 7 new. Need to verify test honesty — would they catch bugs?
before.risk: Low — this is a review, no code changes.

sessions.plan: Run full test suite. Apply deletion test to every changed line. Analyze each new test for false-green patterns. Check Actor integration tests verify side effects not just return values. Write test-report.json and verdict.

actions.context: Read all changed files and tests. Analyzed each test for intent verification vs implementation verification. Applied deletion test to every changed code block.
Wrote all tester outputs: test-report.json with 4 minor findings, PASS verdict, detailed summary with deletion test results.

after.notes: PASS — all 1472 tests green. 7 new tests verify intent correctly. Actor integration tests check disk side effects. 4 minor findings, none blocking.
